title: Publish platform as versioned Go module
body:
## Background

From PR #284 code review findings (`aa9ba571`, `8f06c915`).

## Problem

The `go.mod` file includes a `replace` directive:

```
replace github.com/delightfulhammers/platform => ../platform
```

This:
- Breaks `go install github.com/delightfulhammers/bop/cmd/bop@latest`
- Requires CI to checkout both repos as siblings
- Is brittle if directory layout doesn't match exactly
- Prevents external users from building bop

## Solution

1. Publish `github.com/delightfulhammers/platform` with semantic versioning (e.g., `v0.1.0`)
2. Update bop's `go.mod` to use versioned dependency:
   ```
   require github.com/delightfulhammers/platform v0.1.0
   ```
3. Remove the `replace` directive
4. Developers use `go.work` (already in `.gitignore`) for local cross-module development
5. Simplify CI workflows to not need multi-repo checkout

## Blockers

- Platform needs to stabilize enough for initial version tag
- May need to set up `GOPRIVATE` for private module access

## Files

- `go.mod` - Remove replace directive
- `.github/workflows/ci.yml` - Simplify checkout
- `.github/workflows/code-review.yml` - Simplify checkout
